text: Hide voice mode behind an explicit runtime flag in the default UI
description: Keep the voice integration optional by default in the browser UI. The launcher/runtime config should expose voice only when explicitly enabled, and the default local-first path should hide voice controls and shortcuts.

reflections.context: Keep optional voice integration out of the default first-run UI while preserving a clean opt-in path for people actively working on voice features.
reflections.outcome: Added a runtime-config voice flag, hid the Voice tab and shortcut by default, documented the enablement env vars, and verified the behavior with focused frontend tests plus the local runtime validator.
reflections.effectiveness: High. Reusing the existing runtime-config path avoided touching backend voice routing and kept the change localized to the frontend surface and launcher metadata.
reflections.lesson: For optional features, the highest-value default-path simplification is often to stop advertising the feature before trying to untangle deeper packaging boundaries.
reflections.pattern: This matches other release-prep cleanup where reducing exposed surface area improved first-run clarity without requiring invasive architecture changes.

status.comment: Voice mode is now hidden by default in the browser UI and only appears when the launcher/runtime config or direct frontend env explicitly enables it. Added focused frontend and runtime-validator coverage.
